speaker: Interviewer
text: Karthik, we've been looking at the assembly line data. The speed is high, but the yield is fluctuating quite a bit. What's the primary cause of the rework you're seeing?

speaker: Karthik N.
text: It's almost exclusively the soldering stage. We're hitting a high defect rate in the through-hole soldering, and it’s forcing us into a lot of manual rework. It's killing our margin and slowing down the whole line speed.

speaker: Interviewer
text: How are you currently detecting these soldering defects?

speaker: Karthik N.
text: We have two quality inspectors with magnifying glasses at the end of the line. It's slow and prone to human error. I've been pushing for an AI vision-based inspection system that can catch these defects at high speed, but we haven't found a solution that's reliable enough for our specific board complexity.

speaker: Interviewer
text: If we could automate that detection with 99% accuracy, what would that do to your OEE?

speaker: Karthik N.
text: It would be a game-changer. We could probably increase our throughput by 20% without adding a single person to the floor. The ROI on that vision system would be measured in months, not years.
